Slot: BACK
AC: 5
HP: +10 MANA: +10
SV DISEASE: -10 SV POISON: -10
WT: 0.5 Size: MEDIUM
Class: NEC
Race: HUM ERU DEF GNM IKS FRG DRK
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)

Estate of Unrest
NPC Name
a festering hag

Quests

This item is used in quests.

Neriak Foreign Quarter
Quest Name
Necromancer Words - X`Ta Timpi

Zone(s) Found In:


Zone Name
Estate of Unrest


Uploaded July 3rd, 2022 by halfridge
Send a correction
Post Comment
I don't know
QuoteReply
# Nov 08 2009 at 9:50 PM Rating: Decent
animekenji
1,123 posts
Those negative saves pretty much offset whatever you gain from this item. I mean, it's vastly outdated at this point, but even when it was new I would have been looking at -10 disease and -10 poison and saying nope, not worth it except in rare situations where the saves don't come into play. It's not something I would want to leave in the slot 24/7. I mean, think about it, if you're a necro you're going to be fighting a lot of undead along with necro and sk NPC's that may happen to be in the zone and they use a lot of poison and disease so you'll be needing the saves. I do vaguely recall a few excursions into Unrest long ago where the necros were practically attacking one another for one, though, so they must have had some value once upon a time.



Edited, Nov 8th 2009 11:00pm by animekenji
____________________________
If you were me and I were you, would we still be friends?

read: IT IS CRAP.
QuoteReply
# Sep 15 2002 at 11:06 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591780452900
26 posts
gah, dont you just hate that? it can be a -100 every stat in the game breastplate usable by no class no race and weighs 1000, and some bugger will always defend it! "well its good for a level one character with nothing else to wear and mental problems, at the level you can get it for when you have a full group/guild trying for it." for ***** sake, this item SUCKS. ok, so if it was a newb who never fought anything that used disease or poison, it would...still suck. i think they are actually employees of verant trying to brainwash us. dont listen to them!
▲ Page top
Can't Be Worth Much
QuoteReply
# Apr 13 2002 at 7:43 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592758578638
189 posts
Man looted one of these last night, I, a lvl 38 warrior, and a friend 36 druid, killed about 3 or 4 hags and got it. We went to kill Garanel Rucksif and had to go through these guys to get it,.....Cant be worth much!
38 warrior Teio Littletank
40 druid Bedzilla Darkheart
27 ranger Tazain Blackmontain
26 shadowknight Bednight Soultaker
<Dream Travelers>
▲ Page top
Could be useful
QuoteReply
# Oct 26 2001 at 9:14 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592344474330
Sage
3,705 posts
Ok, look at this cloak this way. Just for a moment, ignore the -10 to save poison and disease, and look at 5ac, 10 mana, 10hp. 5 ac is not bad for a caster back item, and 10 mana is quite a bit for a lower level character, up to maybe 20, if untwinked, 10 hp is extra mana thru cannibalize. I would see this item as one that you might switch out when fighting certain mobs. It only weighs .5, so no biggy to carry in the backpack, and when not fighting mobs with disease or poison attacks, it's actually a pretty nice caster item. Further, even with poison or disease based mobs, it they only have a melee proc type attack, then you're still in good shape, as the pet should keep it off of you.

# Aug 28 2001 at 5:58 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591760700540
67 posts
Aye, it sucks. I sold all the ones I got from Unrest to a merchant (was camping the darkbone bracer for lambent whatchacallit).. Then the next day I go to EC and saw one sell for 100pp. Anyone wanna explain *why* this went for 100, and actually SOLD? I wouldn't pay 2pp for it if I had a necro.. Would take a bare back slot over this thing.
▲ Page top
RE: ...
QuoteReply
# Oct 15 2001 at 2:30 PM Rating: Decent
Ardnahoy
Scholar
340 posts
There are times when a high level player is searching for quest items and will offer insane money to quickly get a crappy item, just to save time. Recently, someone was offering 300pp for a chipped bone rod (or was it a blackened wand - I forget). Did I ask questions? Hell no!

The guy got a guildmate to port to Odus and pick up my lvl 5 chanter alt. The guildmate escorted my alt to Befallen where said dude coughed up 300 shiny coins for a item my main almost threw away.

I know I've overpaid for stuff, just to save time. How many people pay 20pp+ for a port when they could just take a boat ride?
____________________________
Duke Ardnahoy
Noble Lord Protector of the 70th Crusade
Fennin Ro - EQ I

Ardnahoy II
Fearless Guardian of the 40th Tour
Mistmore - EQ II
